author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XVII. 14–19.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While the Lord is still speaking to the Father, and praying for His disciples, He says: “I have given them Thy saying; and the world hath hated them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That hatred they had not yet experienced in those sufferings of their own, which afterwards overtook them; but He speaks thus in His usual way, foretelling the future in words of the past tense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then, subjoining the reason of their being hated by the world, He says, “Because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was conferred on them by regeneration; for by generation they were of the world, as He had already said to them, “I have chosen you out of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was therefore a gracious privilege bestowed upon them, that they, like Himself, should not be of the world, through the deliverance which He was giving them from the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, however, was never of the world; for even in respect of His servant-form He was born of that Holy Spirit of whom they were born again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if on that account they were no more of the world, because born again of the Holy Spirit; on the same account He was never of the world, because born of the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I pray not,” He adds, “that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that Thou shouldest keep them from the evil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they still accounted it necessary to be in the world, although they were no longer of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then He repeats the same statement: “They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sanctify them in the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so are they kept from the evil, as He had previously prayed that they might be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it may be inquired how they were no more of the world, if they were not yet sanctified in the truth; or, if they already were, why He requests that they should be so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not because even those who are sanctified still continue to make progress in the same sanctification, and grow in holiness; and do not so without the aid of God’s grace, but by His sanctifying of their progress, even as He sanctified their outset?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And hence the apostle likewise says: “He who hath begun a good work in you, will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The heirs therefore of the New Testament are sanctified in that truth which was adumbrated in the purifications of the Old Testament; and when they are sanctified in the truth, they are in other words sanctified in Christ, who said in truth, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As also when He said, “The truth shall make you free,” in explanation of His words, He added soon after, “If the Son shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed;” in order to show that what He had previously called the truth, He a minute afterwards denominates the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what else did He mean by the words before us, “Sanctify them in the truth,” but, Sanctify them in me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, He proceeds, and doing so fails not to suggest the same with increasing clearness: “Thy speech (sermo) is truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else did He mean than “I am the truth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Greek Gospel has λόγος, which is also the word that is found in the passage where it is said, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that Word at least we know to be the only begotten Son of God, which “was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence also there might have been put here as it actually has been put in certain copies, “Thy Word is truth;” just as in some copies that other passage is written, “In the beginning was the speech.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in the Greek without any variation it is λόγος in both cases.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father therefore sanctifies in the truth, that is, in His own Word, in His Only begotten, His own heirs and His (the Son’s) co-heirs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He still goes on to speak of the apostles, for He proceeds to add, “As Thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom did He so send but His apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even the very name of apostles, which is a Greek word, signifies in Latin nothing more than, those that are sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God, therefore, sent His Son, not in sinful flesh, but in the likeness of sinful flesh; and His Son sent those who, born themselves in sinful flesh, were sanctified by Him from the defilement of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since, on the ground that the Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, has become Head of the Church, they are His members; therefore He says in the words that follow, “And for their sakes I sanctify myself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what means He by the words, “And for their sakes I sanctify myself,” but I sanctify them in myself, since they also are [part of] myself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those of whom He so speaks are, as I have said, His members; and the head and body are one Christ, as the apostle teaches when he says of the seed of Abraham, “And if ye be Christ’s, then are ye Abraham’s seed,” after having said before, “He saith not, And to seeds, as in many, but as in one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the seed of Abraham is Christ, what else is declared to those to whom he says, “Then are ye Abraham’s seed,” but then are ye Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the same character is what this very apostle said in another place: “Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not, of my afflictions, but “of Christ’s;” for he was a member of Christ, and in his persecutions, such as it behoved Christ to suffer in the whole of His body, he also was filling up his own share of His afflictions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to be assured of the certainty of this in the present passage, give heed to what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after saying, “And for their sakes I sanctify myself,” to let us understand that He thereby meant that He would sanctify them in Himself, He immediately added, “That they also may be sanctified in the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what else is this but in me, in accordance with the fact that the truth is that Word in the beginning which is God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In whom also the Son of man was Himself sanctified from the beginning of His creation, when the Word was made flesh, for the Word and the man became one person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then accordingly He sanctified Himself in Himself, that is, Himself the man in Himself the Word; for the Word and the man is one Christ, who sanctifies the manhood in the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in behalf of His members He says, “And for their sakes I,”—that is, that the benefit may be also theirs, for they too are [included in the] I, just as it benefited me in myself, because I am man apart from them—“I sanctify myself,” that is, I sanctify them as if it were my own self in me, since in me they also are I.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That they also may be sanctified in the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else mean the words “they also,” but [“they”] in the same way as I; “in the truth,” and that “truth” am I?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After this He now begins to speak not only of the apostles, but also of the rest of His members, which we shall treat of, as grace may be granted us, in another discourse.
